summary: I compiled the three review critics' verdicts into a single PR-ready review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`. Rather than taking the prior iteration's claims on trust, I re-verified every substantive assertion against the current code: I confirmed the `requirements-conformance-critic` PASS (all 50 requirements), the `guide-alignment-critic` NEEDS_WORK resting on six minor conformance items, and — importantly — that the `code-quality-critic` verdict is stale (it ran at iteration 1, before the fixes), so I checked all four of its performance/consistency findings and confirmed each is now resolved in the current tree (`broadcast_card.rb` `dashboard:` flag, `recent_activity.rb` per-source DB limits, `sweep.rb` unstuck broadcast, `manager_tick.rb` single fan-out). I also spot-checked that all six open UI findings (F1–F6) are still present so none are misreported as resolved. The report lays out what was asked, what was built, conformance evidence, a Resolved-findings table (with a freshness note explaining the code-quality verdict predates its fixes), an Open-findings table of the six minor items, and a merge recommendation flagging UI-5 (fleet polling) as needing an explicit manager decision and the system tests for CI confirmation.
